subject: Public Health
title: WASH and Economic Development
description: A 60-second lesson on WASH and Economic Development.

hook.text: For every dollar invested in water and sanitation, economies gain $4.30 in returned productivity.

buildup.text: Illness from poor WASH costs low-income countries up to 7% of GDP through healthcare and lost labor.

discovery.text: Children who grow up with clean water and sanitation are healthier, stay in school longer, and earn more as adults.

twist.text: WASH investment pays for itself—yet it consistently ranks among the most underfunded development sectors.

climax.text: The economic case for WASH is clear. The political will to fund it at scale is the missing ingredient.

punchline.text: Clean water doesn't just save lives. It builds economies.

quiz.question: What economic return does every dollar invested in WASH generate?
